quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: Wherever the bishop shall appear, there let the multitude also be; even as, wherever Jesus Christ is, there is the Catholic Church.

quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: See that you all follow the bishop, even as Jesus Christ does the Father, and the presbytery as you would the apostles; and reverence the deacons, as being the institution of God. Let no man do anything connected with the Church without the bishop.

quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: I am the wheat of God, and let me be ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.

quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: Let that be deemed a proper Eucharist, which is [administered] either by the bishop, or by one to whom he has entrusted it.

quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: Abstain from the poison of heretics; for they mix up Jesus Christ with their own poison, speaking things which are unworthy of credit, like those who administer a deadly drug in sweet wine, which he who is ignorant of does greedily take, with a fatal pleasure leading to his own death.

quotes.author: Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.quote: Be on your guard, therefore, against such persons. And this will be the case with you if you are not puffed up, and continue in intimate union with Jesus Christ our God, and the bishop, and the enactments of the apostles. He that is within the altar is pure, but he that is without is not pure; that is, he who does anything apart from the bishop, and presbytery, and deacons, such a man is not pure in his conscience.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: For He was made man that we might be made God.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: For He has become Man, that He might deify us in Himself, and He has been born of a woman, and begotten of a Virgin, in order to transfer to Himself our erring generation, and that we may become henceforth a holy race, and 'partakers of the Divine Nature,' as blessed Peter wrote.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: But, beyond these sayings, let us look at the very tradition, teaching, and faith of the Catholic Church from the beginning, which the Lord gave, the Apostles preached, and the Fathers kept. Upon this the Church is founded, and he who should fall away from it would not be a Christian, and should no longer be so called.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: For when the great prayer and the holy invocation have been made, the Word descends upon the bread and the cup, and it becomes His body.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: …when baptism is given, whom the Father baptizes, him the Son baptizes; and whom the Son baptizes, he is consecrated in the Holy Ghost.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: But this modern and accursed heresy [Arianism], when it is overthrown by argument, when it is cast down and covered with shame by the very Truth, immediately endeavours to coerce by violence and stripes and imprisonment those whom it has been unable to persuade by argument, thereby acknowledging itself to be anything rather than godly.

quotes.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
quotes.quote: Vainly then, here again, O Arians, have you made this conjecture, and vainly alleged the words of Scripture; for God's Word is unalterable, and is ever in one state, not as it may happen, but as the Father is; since how is He like the Father, unless He be thus?

quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.quote: Never separate yourself from the Church. For nothing is stronger than the Church. Your hope is the Church alone; your salvation is inside the Church only, your refuge is the Church. She is higher than the heavens, and wider than the whole earth. She never grows old, but is always full of vigor and vitality. Holy Scripture, when pointing to her strength and stability, calls her an unshakable mountain ...

quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.quote: For when you see the Lord sacrificed, and laid upon the altar, and the priest standing and praying over the victim, and all the worshippers empurpled with that precious blood, can you then think that you are still among men, and standing upon the earth? Are you not, on the contrary, straightway translated to Heaven?

quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.quote: “I reminded you many times about the atheist heretics (non-Orthodox), and now I implore you not to compromise with them on anything, do not eat or drink with them in the name of friendship, better relations, love or peace, because he who is swayed and compromises with them renders himself foreign to the Catholic (universal) church.”

quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.quote: Be aware not to be corrupted from love of the heretics; for this reason do not accept any false belief (dogma) in the name of love.

quotes.author: John Chrysostom
quotes.quote: Nothing so provokes God’s anger as the division of the Church.

quotes.author: John of Damascus
quotes.quote: The bread and the wine are not merely figures of the body and blood of Christ (God forbid!) but the deified body of the Lord itself.

quotes.author: John of Damascus
quotes.quote: When the Invisible One becomes visible to flesh, you may then draw His likeness.

quotes.author: John of Damascus
quotes.quote: I do not worship matter, I worship the Creator of matter who became matter for my sake, who willed to take His abode in matter, and who worked out my salvation through matter.

quotes.author: John of Damascus
quotes.quote: Therefore I venture to draw an image of the invisible God, not as invisible, but as having become visible for our sakes through flesh and blood. I do not draw an image of the immortal Godhead. I paint the visible flesh of God, for it is impossible to represent a spirit, how much more God who gives breath to the spirit.

quotes.author: John of Damascus
quotes.quote: I see the Church which God founded on the Apostles and Prophets, its corner-stone being Christ His Son, tossed on an angry sea, beaten by rushing waves, shaken and troubled by the assaults of evil spirits.

quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.quote: The leaders of any given evil heresy also attempt to interpret their writings, but their purpose is to harm their followers and deny that truth which is in accordance with piety, using the words of the Spirit against the Spirit.

quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.quote: Deification is an enhypostatic and direct illumination which has no beginning, but appears in those worthy as something exceeding their comprehension. It is indeed mystical union with God, beyond intellect and reason, in the age when creatures will no longer know corruption.

quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.quote: Moreover, the transformation of our human nature, its deification and transfiguration - were these not accomplished in Christ from the start, from the moment in which He assumedour nature?

quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.quote: Since the Son of God, in his incomparable love for man, did not only unite His divine Hypostasis with our nature, by clothing Himself in a living body and a soul gifted with intelligence… but also united himself… with the human hypostases themselves, in mingling himself with each of the faithful by communion with his Holy Body, and since he becomes one single body with us

quotes.author: Gregory Palamas
quotes.quote: For, on the day of the Transfiguration, that Body, source of the light of grace, was not yet united with our bodies, it illuminated from outside those who worthily approached it, and sent the illumination into the soul by an intermediary of the physical eyes; but now, since it mingled with us and exists in us, it illuminates the soul from within.
